content: YAML parser implementation: greenhouse uses a custom minimal YAML parser (src/config.ts parseYaml) supporting nested objects, string arrays, object arrays, block scalars (|), booleans, numbers. Based on overstory pattern but extends with block scalar support for pr_template multiline strings.

JSONL state persistence: state.ts uses append-only JSONL with dedup-on-read, advisory file locking (exclusive create + 30s stale detection), and atomic temp-file-then-rename writes. Same pattern as seeds/mulch.
Daily budget derived from state: budget.ts counts RunState entries where dispatchedAt date matches today (YYYY-MM-DD prefix). No separate budget file; state.jsonl is the single source of truth.

Infrastructure file set for greenhouse: README.md (install, quickstart, CLI reference, design principles, os-eco links), CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format), CONTRIBUTING.md (dev setup, quality gates, code style, testing, versioning), LICENSE (MIT 2026 Jaymin West), CODEOWNERS (* @jayminwest), SECURITY.md (report to jayminwest@gmail.com 48h response), .github/workflows/ci.yml (exact SPEC.md match), .github/workflows/publish.yml (package.json path trigger, bunx npm publish, auto-tag, gh release), scripts/version-bump.ts (updates package.json + src/cli.ts VERSION constant), .claude/commands/release.md (quality gates, bump, changelog, commit, push)

All pipeline module functions (pollIssues, ingestIssue, dispatchRun, checkRunStatus, shipRun) accept an optional ExecFn parameter for subprocess injection, enabling unit tests without real CLI calls. defaultExec uses Bun.spawn.

Label mapping from GH labels to seeds fields: priority:P0-P4 -> 0-4, type:bug/feature/task -> seeds type, area:X -> '[X] ' description prefix, difficulty:X -> ' (X)' suffix. Unmapped labels preserved in description. biome.json for worktrees inside .overstory must use includes:['src/**'] not ['**'] to avoid excluding itself.
state.ts appendFile/writeFile require parent directory to exist — use mkdir({recursive:true}) on the dirname of the state path before writing. appendRun and writeAllRuns both need this guard.

When merging foundation and pipeline branches with divergent APIs, adopt the test-expected signature as the canonical one and update all call sites. Aliases (readState = readAllRuns) and additional standalone functions (computeBudget, budgetExhausted, getDailyBudget) are the right way to satisfy both test suites without removing existing functionality.

Greenfield build decomposition: 4 parallel leads (foundation, pipeline, CLI, infra) with non-overlapping file areas. Merge order: foundation first (base types), then others. Auto-resolve merges may require fixup leads when parallel leads create overlapping files like types.ts.

pid.ts module pattern: pidFilePath(projectRoot?) returns .greenhouse/daemon.pid path; writePid/readPid use Bun.file; isProcessAlive uses process.kill(pid,0); removePid swallows ENOENT. start.ts detach: spawn child sans --detach flag with detached:true + log fd redirect; stop.ts: SIGTERM then poll 250ms intervals up to 10s before error.

CLI command pattern for runs.ts: export testable pure functions (listRuns, findRunsByIssueId, cancelRun, retryRun) alongside Commander action registration. Actions use program.opts().json for global --json flag and setJsonMode() before any output. For retry, load config to find repoConfig and call dispatchRun; fall back to pending-only reset if config unavailable.
